clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for pressure area skin review.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common Dermatology documentation scenario suitable as clinician-controlled note scaffolding rather than autonomous clinical advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct documentation workflow for clinician-selected details around pressure area skin review.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Exact ID must be checked absent from existing_workflow_ids.json; nearest relevant baseline concept is derm-skin-lesion-review. Candidate is retained only if aliases and purpose remain distinct.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
red_flag_prompts: rapid spread, mucosal involvement, or systemic symptoms documented if assessed
secondary infection signs documented if assessed
severe pain or immunocompromise documented if relevant

workflow_identity.evidence_relationship: The exact risk and skin-assessment section supports documenting a pressure-area review, onset or interval change, function and mobility impact, relevant risk context, clinician skin inspection, distribution or site, general appearance, pain, and previous records without assigning a risk score or diagnosis.

clinical_blockers: Exact location, skin tone, mobility, devices, nutrition, moisture, sensation, risk tool, lesion dimensions, category, depth, infection, diagnosis, prevention plan, equipment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
